diff_summary: Add ac_020_sequential_only + ac_020_concurrent_only #[ignore] tests gated by AC020_PHASE env var; combined gate unchanged
unexpected_observations: Pre-existing compile errors in tests/compatibility.rs, tests/cursors.rs, tests/lifecycle_observability.rs — not caused by this phase, agent-verify.sh green regardless. Combined gate bound assertion fails (concurrent=118ms > bound=34ms) — pre-existing regression, not introduced here.
data_for_pivot: Sequential 184ms and concurrent 118ms in same process suggests contention is already present in the engine-level lock. If A.1 perf capture shows the profile shape differs between split and combined runs, inline both phases into a single test process to control for JIT warmup and kernel scheduler effects. Key symbol to look for: the WAL checkpoint or connection-pool mutex.

downstream_invocations.sequential: AC020_PHASE=sequential cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --release --test perf_gates -- --ignored ac_020_sequential_only --nocapture
downstream_invocations.concurrent: AC020_PHASE=concurrent cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --release --test perf_gates -- --ignored ac_020_concurrent_only --nocapture
